1.speaker: female
1.text: Oh my god, no way.

1.speaker: male
1.text: I was literally gonna text you yesterday.

1.speaker: female
1.text: Liar.

1.speaker: male
1.text: Okay, I thought about texting you. That counts.

1.speaker: female
1.text: That does not count and you know it.

1.speaker: male
1.text: You cut your hair.

1.speaker: female
1.text: Three months ago.

1.speaker: male
1.text: Well it looks new to me.

1.speaker: female
1.text: That's because you don't look at anyone, that's a whole other conversation.

1.speaker: male
1.text: Are we doing this on the sidewalk or —

1.speaker: female
1.text: We are absolutely doing this on the sidewalk.

1.speaker: male
1.text: Did your sister end up moving?

1.speaker: female
1.text: She did, yeah. Portland. She hates it already.

1.speaker: male
1.text: Tell her I said called it.

1.speaker: female
1.text: I will not be passing that along.

1.speaker: male
1.text: Fair. Hey — are you free Sunday-ish?

1.speaker: female
1.text: Sunday-ish meaning what, like noon, or like you'll text me at four and bail at five?

1.speaker: male
1.text: … noon.

1.speaker: female
1.text: Text me Saturday so I believe you.

1.speaker: male
1.text: Deal. Go, your coffee's getting sad.

2.speaker: female
2.text: Nice.

2.speaker: male
2.text: Well, this is awkward..

2.speaker: female
2.text: Oh, was it? Not ditch and diner on me?

2.speaker: male
2.text: hey there, I am just trying to win this game.

2.speaker: female
2.text: I will make sure you are not going to win.

2.speaker: male
2.text: Like you can do it.

2.speaker: female
2.text: Did you realized that I'm your sister's friend, right?

2.speaker: male
2.text: Mhmm....is that so?

2.speaker: female
2.text: That is so.

2.speaker: male
2.text: Let's see...how about this? I pay you after the game?

2.speaker: female
2.text: And how can I trust you?

2.speaker: male
2.text: Good question.

2.speaker: female
2.text: You are a horrible, did you know that, right?

2.speaker: male
2.text: Mm. Not really

2.speaker: female
2.text: You think it was ok to do that?

2.speaker: male
2.text: Ah.I mean, if you didn't order expensive food...

2.speaker: female
2.text: Yeah.

2.speaker: male
2.text: So..how did you manage to get out?

2.speaker: female
2.text: Your dad paid for me.

2.speaker: male
2.text: No way!

3.speaker: female
3.text: You seriously brought a calculator to mini golf?

3.speaker: male
3.text: It's called strategy. Tiger Woods would've respected this.

3.speaker: female
3.text: Tiger Woods would tell you to touch grass.

3.speaker: male
3.text: We are literally standing on grass.

3.speaker: female
3.text: Fake grass. Which honestly matches your confidence level right now.

3.speaker: male
3.text: Bold words for someone losing by six.

3.speaker: female
3.text: I'm not losing. I'm creating suspense.

3.speaker: male
3.text: The suspense is whether your ball will ever leave the windmill.

3.speaker: female
3.text: The windmill and I have formed an emotional bond.

3.speaker: male
3.text: I can see that. It keeps rejecting you gently.

3.speaker: female
3.text: You're enjoying this way too much.

3.speaker: male
3.text: I haven't felt joy since 2019, this is huge for me.

3.speaker: female
3.text: Wow. Do you always flirt like a divorced substitute teacher?

3.speaker: male
3.text: Only on special occasions.

3.speaker: female
3.text: Careful, I might start thinking this is a date.

3.speaker: male
3.text: If this was a date I'd be pretending to let you win.

3.speaker: female
3.text: You're saying you're naturally this annoying?

3.speaker: male
3.text: Actually I'm toning it down.

3.speaker: female
3.text: Terrifying.

3.speaker: male
3.text: Hey, if I buy you fries after this, will you stop trash talking me?

3.speaker: female
3.text: No. But I will upgrade to affectionate bullying.

4.speaker: female
4.text: We have been on step 4 for forty minutes.

4.speaker: male
4.text: I'm in the zone.

4.speaker: female
4.text: This is not a zone. This is a hostage situation.

4.speaker: male
4.text: The instructions are wrong.

4.speaker: female
4.text: The instructions came with the bookcase.

4.speaker: male
4.text: And yet they're wrong.

4.speaker: female
4.text: You're holding them upside down.

4.speaker: female
4.text: How long.

4.speaker: male
4.text: I will not be answering that question.

4.speaker: female
4.text: Step 4 makes way more sense now.

4.speaker: male
4.text: Don't. Don't enjoy this.

4.speaker: female
4.text: I'm going to enjoy this for the rest of my life.

4.speaker: male
4.text: Cool. I'll just go live in the woods now.

4.speaker: female
4.text: Hand me the screws.

4.speaker: male
4.text: You don't even know which ones —

4.speaker: female
4.text: The little ones with the weird heads.

4.speaker: male
4.text: Damn it.

4.speaker: female
4.text: Yeah. That's right. I read the manual.

4.speaker: male
4.text: When?

4.speaker: female
4.text: While you were "in the zone."

5.speaker: female
5.text: You're about to do something stupid.

5.speaker: female
5.text: You always smile like that before bad decisions.

5.speaker: female
5.text: This is your "I just put my name in for Bohemian Rhapsody" smile.

5.speaker: male
5.text: …I don't know what you're talking about.

5.speaker: female
5.text: You did it. You actually did it.

5.speaker: female
5.text: Your name is on the screen.

5.speaker: female
5.text: It says your full name. With the middle name.

5.speaker: male
5.text: Lots of people are named that.

5.speaker: female
5.text: Your mom is not getting on stage tonight.

5.speaker: female
5.text: You have ninety seconds before they call you.

6.speaker: female
6.text: There is no -ish. Five is five.

6.speaker: male
6.text: -ish is doing a lot of heavy lifting in our friendship.

6.speaker: male
6.text: Has it? Wow. Time flies when you're being judged.

6.speaker: male
6.text: You're literally side-eyeing me right now.

6.speaker: male
6.text: Because I told her you'd be here.

6.speaker: female
6.text: I'm not getting out of this car.

7.speaker: male
7.text: That's not your dog, that's mine.

7.speaker: female
7.text: That's mine. The leash is mine.

7.speaker: male
7.text: Then your leash is on my dog.

7.speaker: male
7.text: I don't know, I was on my phone.

7.speaker: male
7.text: Cool. So we both failed our one job.

7.speaker: female
7.text: Do they look enough alike that nobody noticed?

7.speaker: male
7.text: Yeah, no one's giving us awards today.

7.speaker: female
7.text: Sorry, weirdest 30 seconds of my morning.

8.speaker: female
8.text: I had a dream about you.

8.speaker: female
8.text: A really big horse. Like, scary big.

8.speaker: male
8.text: Why are you telling me this.

8.speaker: female
8.text: I had to. I just woke up.

8.speaker: male
8.text: This is what the notes app is for.

8.speaker: female
8.text: I tried. I couldn't go back to sleep.

9.speaker: female
9.text: They keep asking how long we've been together.

9.speaker: male
9.text: Because every time I do they look more confused.

9.speaker: male
9.text: I tried that. The aunt cried.

9.speaker: male
9.text: I think she really wanted us to be married.

9.speaker: female
9.text: Maybe we should just… go with it.

9.speaker: female
9.text: We met at a — coffee shop.

9.speaker: male
9.text: We met during a hostage situation.

9.speaker: female
9.text: We are not telling people that.

10.speaker: male
10.text: I thought you brought your keys.

10.speaker: female
10.text: Why would I bring my keys, we left from your place.

10.speaker: male
10.text: We left from MY place, that doesn't mean —

10.speaker: male
10.text: Okay, fine, I'll call the locksmith.

10.speaker: male
10.text: I can see your breath also, that's how breath works.

10.speaker: male
10.text: I'm calling a locksmith, that's helping.

10.speaker: female
10.text: Do you have anything warm in the car.

11.speaker: female
11.text: That's because you're driving and I'm staring at corn.

11.speaker: male
11.text: How many snacks does one person need.

11.speaker: female
11.text: All of them. I need all of them.

11.speaker: male
11.text: We have an entire bag of pretzels.

11.speaker: female
11.text: Pretzels are not a snack, they are a personality flaw.
